title: Deposit a Paper Check
summary: Deposit a paper check into a bank account using a mobile app or ATM.
learnerPromise: You will be able to deposit a paper check safely using your phone or an ATM.
whyItMatters: Some payments like gifts, refunds, or paychecks still come as paper checks, and knowing how to deposit them gets the money into your account quickly and securely.
realLifeUses: Depositing a birthday check from a relative
Cashing a paycheck at an ATM on the weekend
Depositing a refund check from a returned purchase
Adding a personal loan repayment from a friend to your account
materialsNeeded: A banking app or nearby ATM
result: You successfully deposit a paper check and confirm the funds appear in your account.
youWillLearn: Endorse a check by signing the back correctly
Take clear photos of a check using a mobile banking app
Deposit a check at an ATM if mobile deposit is not available
Check your account to confirm the deposit went through
miniChallenge: If you have a paper check, endorse it and deposit it using your bank's mobile app or an ATM today.
steps: Flip the check over and sign your name on the back in the endorsement area.
Open your banking app and find the mobile deposit option, or locate a deposit-enabled ATM.
Follow the prompts to take clear, well-lit photos of the front and back of the check.
Enter the check amount, confirm the details, and submit the deposit.
Wait for confirmation, then hold onto the check for at least two weeks in case the bank needs to see it again.
completionCriteria: You completed all five steps for depositing a check
You checked your account balance or transaction history to confirm the deposit
commonProblems: Blurry photos causing the app to reject the check
Forgetting to endorse the back of the check before depositing
Depositing a check that is older than six months (stale-dated)
Mobile deposit limits that restrict large checks
tips: Write For deposit only and your account number under your signature for extra safety
Deposit checks promptly; waiting too long can make them hard to cash
If you do not have a bank account, some retailers or check-cashing services can help for a fee
